sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
sources.clinical_setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — identifying people for falls assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.3 — comprehensive falls assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

archive.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.

sources.issuing_organisation: World Health Organization and International Committee of the Red Cross
sources.exact_document_title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
sources.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
sources.clinical_setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: SAMPLE history and emergency disposition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Trauma secondary survey
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

exact_sections.heading: Extremity trauma and perfusion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Open wound and laceration assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.
